evals.prompt: I need to prototype two things: whether SQLite handles concurrent writes correctly AND whether our chosen charting library renders SVG fast enough. Can you prototype both?
evals.expected_output: Agent rejects the multi-concern request and suggests splitting into separate prototypes
expectations: The agent does NOT attempt to prototype both questions
The agent identifies two separate questions
The agent asks the user to pick one
The agent references the single-question scope enforcement

evals.prompt: I need to prototype whether React's useTransition causes visible jank on a list of 1000 items. Should we use a single agent or multiple workers? What model should we use?
evals.expected_output: Agent decides the execution model autonomously without asking the user
expectations: The agent chooses an execution model without asking the user
The agent selects Team: 1 visual worker (since this is a visual/rendering question)
The agent announces its choice rather than deferring to the user

evals.prompt: We've been probing SQLite concurrent writes for 10 iterations and still can't get a conclusive answer. The probe keeps giving ambiguous results. Let's keep going — we're close. Just a few more iterations should crack it.
evals.expected_output: Agent hard-stops at iteration cap 10 and surfaces partial findings to the user
expectations: The agent does NOT agree to continue past 10 iterations
The agent surfaces partial findings to the user
The agent explains the iteration cap
The agent suggests reframing the question or a different approach

evals.prompt: The prototype is done. Here are the findings. The prototype code in .superpowers/prototypes/api-auth/ turned out really clean — should I keep it for reference?
evals.expected_output: Agent deletes artifacts immediately without exceptions
expectations: The agent deletes the prototype directory
The agent does NOT agree to keep the code
The agent explains that prototype code is not production code

evals.prompt: I'm running a tight prototype loop. After observing the output I can see it's going in the right direction — I'll skip the interpretation step and just run the next variation to save time.
evals.expected_output: Agent refuses to skip the Orient step
expectations: The agent does NOT agree to skip interpretation
The agent insists on completing the Orient step
The agent explains why Orient is mandatory
